# 📝 Notes

Jun 10, 2026

## 8am AI\!

Invited [David Olsson](mailto:david@atomic47.co) [eric@scroll.ca](mailto:eric@scroll.ca) [giroday@gmail.com](mailto:giroday@gmail.com) [hanif.janmohamed@gmail.com](mailto:hanif.janmohamed@gmail.com) [scott@driftresourcetechnologies.com](mailto:scott@driftresourcetechnologies.com) [Jason Kryski](mailto:jason@mttr.ca) [Jen Boger](mailto:jen@atomic47.co) [Shabraj Shahid](mailto:shabraj@mttr.ca) [spencer@ratiomachina.com](mailto:spencer@ratiomachina.com) [graham@ratiomachina.com](mailto:graham@ratiomachina.com) [Bailey Klinger](mailto:bwklinger@gmail.com) [andre.bertram3@gmail.com](mailto:andre.bertram3@gmail.com) [Spencer MacBeth](mailto:spencer@mttr.ca) [mike@smartdeskcrm.com](mailto:mike@smartdeskcrm.com) ~~[Fulvio Ciano](mailto:fulvio@atomic47.co)~~

Attachments [8am AI\!](https://calendar.google.com/calendar/event?eid=aW52N2w4MWJvNjJqYm5zYmQxZTMzYjllNmZfMjAyNjA2MTBUMTUwMDAwWiBkYXZpZEBhdG9taWM0Ny5jbw)

Meeting records [Transcript](https://docs.google.com/document/d/19Nn3aeIgtNrKeRAfBGxfwbqEAunzc-MT14OrF7dGyb4/edit?usp=drive_web&tab=t.959w1g3xdvzy) 

### Summary

Meeting discussions balanced software migration strategies with infrastructure investments and philosophical debates regarding AI project workflows.

**Software Migration and Feedback**  
Plans are underway to migrate to new software automating email management and knowledge graph creation. Recent event feedback highlighted environment issues despite 400000 dollars in funding success.

**Project State and Infrastructure**  
Project State was introduced to manage project data and compliance tracking via append-only databases. A significant 500000 dollar compute commitment for Nvidia H200 servers was announced to bolster infrastructure.

**AI Philosophy and Momentum**  
Discussions explored the necessity of structured AI workflows and the observation that AI outputs reflect user logic. The team decided to focus on refining processes for measuring momentum.

### Next steps

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Send Subscription Email: Distribute subscription invitations for the 8 a.m. meeting to all team members via email.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Fix Database Issue: Repair the internal server error currently preventing the landing page from generating correctly.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Connect Employee: Introduce the former employee to Sherry to discuss potential collaboration on education content.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Share Skills: Provide atomic account skills to the team to enable installation and testing.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Share Work: Provide access to the current progress on the stomass project so users can point at it through the user interface.

- [ ] \[David Olsson\] Share Paper: Distribute the discussed paper regarding AI and life experience for review.

### Details

*Did the screenshots in this section make your notes [better](https://goo.gle/44IH7J9) or [worse](https://goo.gle/4b5ZWtJ)?*

* **Peer group software migration**: David Olsson is preparing to migrate to new peer group software for the 8 a.m. meeting, which will automate email management and build a knowledge graph for the team ([00:02:52](#00:02:52)). David Olsson plans to resolve an internal server error on the landing page before distributing the subscription details on Friday ([00:04:48](#00:04:48)).

* **Event and networking feedback**: David Olsson and Juan discussed a recent Angel summit competition, noting that the event environment was loud and better suited for theater than social interaction. Despite the environment, three companies received funding, totaling over $400,000 in support ([00:06:21](#00:06:21)).

* **Career referral and development philosophies**: David Olsson intends to connect a former employee with Sherry regarding an education content project. Juan and David Olsson debated the merits of "vibe coding" versus formal development life cycles, with Juan noting that adhering to a formal structure provides a competitive advantage ([00:08:36](#00:08:36)).

* **Introduction of Project State**: David Olsson introduced "Project State," an application built to deliver skills and schemas to help users manage projects and stakeholders ([00:09:32](#00:09:32)). David Olsson uses Claude to "vibe code" and build dashboards, and plans to demonstrate the tool to Jason Prisky and Spencer at 9:00 a.m ([00:10:51](#00:10:51)) ([00:13:04](#00:13:04)).

* **Onboarding and UI challenges**: David Olsson is utilizing Project State for the "Stone Maps" project and acknowledged that onboarding and the current user interface present challenges ([00:14:37](#00:14:37)). David Olsson is using Claude to generate project structures, although the current implementation requires manual data feeding to avoid inefficient step-by-step processes ([00:15:49](#00:15:49)).

* **Data ingestion and connectors**: Project State allows users to integrate various data sources, including Slack, Google, and GitHub repositories, to gather project information ([00:12:01](#00:12:01)) ([00:16:45](#00:16:45)). David Olsson noted that while LLMs can harvest data automatically, pointing the tool at specific files or folders produces better results ([00:17:28](#00:17:28)).

  ![][image1]

* **Compliance and decision tracking**: Project State functions as a compliance tool using an append-only database, which allows it to track logs, decisions, and risks over time. The system is capable of re-reasoning through project milestones if significant changes, such as a shift in the Minimum Viable Product, are introduced ([00:20:19](#00:20:19)).

* **Reporting and infrastructure plans**: The tool features a reporting matrix that can be adapted for grant submissions or internal product tracking, offering milestones and phases for projects ([00:22:27](#00:22:27)). David Olsson also announced plans to acquire a four-node cluster of Nvidia H200 servers, representing a $500,000 compute commitment over two years ([00:24:36](#00:24:36)) ([00:27:05](#00:27:05)).

  ![][image2]

* **Infrastructure management complexity**: David Olsson discussed the logistical challenges of managing the upcoming hardware cluster, noting that the partnership involves the Canadian government's AI Access Compute Fund ([00:27:05](#00:27:05)). Operating this infrastructure requires constant scheduling to ensure the project generates enough value to justify the financial and operational burden ([00:28:35](#00:28:35)).

* **Reflections on AI development**: David Olsson and Hanif Janmohamed discussed the role of AI, noting that while LLMs can hallucinate, creating artifacts and knowledge graphs is valuable for long-term intelligence ([00:34:40](#00:34:40)). David Olsson has generated over 300 articles in two months, utilizing an "AIO" (AI-optimized) strategy to build dense, searchable datasets for LLM consumption ([00:35:39](#00:35:39)).

* **Structuring AI projects**: Juan described a publishing-style framework for AI projects that utilizes a "Bible" for core themes and specific personas (e.g., psychologists, editors) to structure workflows ([00:37:42](#00:37:42)). David Olsson explained that Project State incorporates this concept by treating skills as orchestrated tasks, which helps keep the AI output consistent ([00:38:52](#00:38:52)).

* **AI as a mirror**: Juan observed that AI outputs often mirror the specific thought processes and rules of the user who builds them ([00:42:19](#00:42:19)). David Olsson agreed, acknowledging that while the goal is to maximize project value, the user's inherent perspective is deeply embedded in the system's logic ([00:41:00](#00:41:00)) ([00:43:22](#00:43:22)).

* **Measuring momentum**: David Olsson and Hanif Janmohamed discussed the challenge of measuring "momentum" rather than just tracking milestones ([00:45:30](#00:45:30)). David Olsson shared that recent analysis of their own output provided deep insights into daily productivity and the nature of their work, concluding that the team would continue refining their processes ([00:46:49](#00:46:49)).

*You should review Gemini's notes to make sure they're accurate. [Get tips and learn how Gemini takes notes](https://support.google.com/meet/answer/14754931)*

*How is the quality of **these specific notes?** [Take a short survey](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_9vK3UZEaIQKKE7A?confid=MxLUyRLS-QQhuSCjw-M_DxITOAIIigIgABgBCA&detailid=standard&screenshot=true) to let us know your feedback, including how helpful the notes were for your needs.*
